title: Rhubarb custard pie
subtitle: A tart rhubarb filling set in a vanilla custard, in a blind-baked pastry case
excerpt: Rhubarb custard pie pairs the sharpness of spring rhubarb with a sweet, lightly set vanilla custard. The rhubarb goes in raw, the custard is poured over, and the whole thing bakes together until the custard is just set and the rhubarb is tender. The pastry case is blind-baked to prevent a soggy base.
sourceNotes: Rhubarb custard pie is documented in American and British home-baking literature from the nineteenth century. The combination of raw rhubarb baked with a custard filling is a well-established technique in both traditions.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pie needs at least 3 hours to cool and set. Keeps in the fridge for 3 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan: 170 degrees C for blind bake; reduce to 160 degrees C conventional for the custard

glossaryTerms.definition: Custard pie fillings are liquid when they go into the pastry case, and a raw pastry base will absorb moisture from the custard and go soggy before it has a chance to set. Blind-baking, which means baking the empty pastry case with baking beans to hold the sides up, pre-bakes and partially sets the base before the custard is added. This creates a moisture barrier that keeps the base crisp even after the custard sets.

content.text: The balance in this pie is between the sharpness of rhubarb and the sweetness of the custard. The rhubarb is cut into pieces and laid raw in the pastry case; it releases juice during baking but the surrounding custard absorbs most of it.
the pastry case before adding the filling is not optional; a raw base will always be soggy. The custard should be barely set when the pie comes out of the oven; it firms as it cools.

items.prepNote: total; 60 g for rhubarb, 120 g for custard

content.text: Mix the flour and icing sugar. Rub in the cold butter to a breadcrumb texture. Mix in the egg yolk and cold water until the dough just comes together. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 190 degrees C (conventional). Roll to line a 23 cm pie dish. Line with baking paper, fill with baking beans, and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper and bake for 5 more minutes. Reduce the oven to 160 degrees C (conventional).

Toss the rhubarb pieces with 60 g of the caster sugar and leave to stand for 10 minutes. Whisk together the remaining 120 g sugar, flour, eggs, cream, and vanilla until smooth. Arrange the rhubarb in the warm pastry case. Pour the custard over the rhubarb; it will fill in around and between the pieces. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes until the custard is just set with a gentle wobble in the centre and the rhubarb is tender when pierced. Cool on a rack for at least 3 hours before slicing.
